subject: Computer Science
coverageNote: Form directories exist only where teaching-module source PDFs were ingested. Missing forms must resolve as known-gap, not empty objects.

lesson.title: 1: PRODUCTIVITY TOOLS
topics: This topic introduces learners to essential productivity tools, with a primary focus on spreadsheets.
Learners will understand how software applications enhance efficiency in organising, calculating,
analysing, and presenting data across various contexts such as schools, offices, and homes. By the end
of this topic, learners will be able to perform fundamental data entry, analysis, and visualisation tasks.
activities: Choose any of the following but not limited to:
Classroom: Use board or charts to discuss spreadsheet components.
Charts and Posters: Display labelled spreadsheet parts.
Hands-On Demo: Learners explore spreadsheet software on computers.
Computer Laboratory: Learners interact directly with spreadsheet tools.
assessment: Scenario: Your cousin Bwalya runs a small kantemba at Soweto Market in Lusaka. She sells vegetables such
as tomatoes, onions, and cabbage. She has an exercise book where she writes each day’s sales, but at the
end of the week, she spends hours adding everything with a calculator and often makes mistakes. She has
heard that a computer can help her.
(Data Entry & Interface) You open a blank spreadsheet for Bwalya. She looks at all the empty boxes
